steps.title: Recognize the comma placement
steps.content: ”This period is remembered for its grandeur and artistry,” is an independent clause, followed by a comma and “its vehicles ______ by collectors.” The words after the comma will determine whether we create a comma splice.

steps.title: Choose the option that avoids a second independent clause
steps.content: Choice C uses “highly prized” as an adjective without an auxiliary verb, so “its vehicles highly prized by collectors” forms a reduced relative clause, not an independent clause. Choices A, B, and D each contain a form of “to be” (are, had been, were), creating a second independent clause and producing a comma splice. That matches choice C — C is correct.
